content: The graded vocabulary acquisition framework for teaching 'reflexion' as a flexible noun/verb in high school English can be repurposed to improve LLM self-reflection mechanisms, by structuring reflection outputs along progressively nuanced proficiency levels, just as language learners build from basic definition recall to sophisticated reflective writing. This structured proficiency scaling addresses a key gap in current LLM self-reflection which often produces unstructured, inconsistent feedback across reasoning tasks.
